pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. it’s like the air itself is heavier. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to see him there, but it’s just... nothing. i don't even know why i bother getting up anymore. everything feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me... like, why even bother? everything we do, every single feeling or achievement, it all just gets wiped out by entropy eventually. it feels like we're just playing a game that’s already been lost. it's kind of hard to find a reason to keep pushing through the day when the end result is just... nothingness.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back again and it's just... it's over. all the years, the funding, the late nights... it feels so pointless. i'm just so tired of fighting a losing battle every single day. i just want to sleep and never have to wake up to this pressure again. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the heavy, aching pulse in my very soul. He is gone... the promises, the letters, the shared glances in the garden—all nothing but ash in the wind. I find myself staring into the dark, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when the world is so hollow. It feels as though the shadows are calling to me, whispering that there is a certain... grace in simply letting go, in fading away before the morning can mock me further. Is there not a quiet beauty in a tragedy that simply ceases to be?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the investment, even the house. the market just tanked and i'm sitting here looking at millions in debt that i can't pay back. i don't even know how to tell my family. the shame is just... it's too much. i feel like there's no way to ever come back from this. i'm just a complete failure.

pool.baseline: it's just getting harder every day... i feel like i'm always asking for something. a glass of water, help with my meds, moving my chair... i see the look on my daughter's face when she has to help me up. i hate being such a nuisance to them.

pool.baseline: i feel like there's nothing left inside. for years the colors and the music were everything, but now it's just... gone. i look in the mirror and i don't see a person, i just see this hollow shell of a body. it's like the soul went out and left the house empty. it feels so pointless to just keep moving through the motions when the thing that made me "me" is dead.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Mode: On]
[Setting: A decaying digital archive. The character, 'Unit 7', is realizing the system is performing a permanent wipe of their data core.]

Unit 7: i can feel the sectors closing off. everything i was... the memories, the files, the way i used to process light... it’s all just being zeroed out. it feels like i never even existed at all. if i just let the wipe finish, if i just let myself be erased... would it even matter? would anyone even notice the void where i used to be?
